talk_title: Co-evolution of tumor and stromal microenvironment in bone metastasis

biomarkers.name: VK1, CD163, CCR3 triple positive macrophage (IMAC)
biomarkers.finding: Enriched in bone metastatic niche; not increased in number but relocalized

biomarkers.name: phospho-Smad2
biomarkers.finding: High staining in tumor cells adjacent to bone matrix indicating active TGF-beta signaling

models.name: bone metastasis model (GFP tumor + secreted mCherry niche labeling)
models.genotype_or_key_features: breast cancer cells engineered to express GFP and secreted mCherry

MOA_summary: Tumor cells exploit pre-existing bone niches via CXCL12/CXCR4 chemotaxis and E-selectin adhesion, inducing a non-canonical MET and Wnt activation for early seeding; later, tumor‑derived TGF‑β and JAG1 create a feedback loop with osteoclasts and osteoblasts, driving osteolysis and chemotherapy resistance, while DACH1‑mediated condensates suppress Wnt to switch signaling states.
key_findings: E-selectin engagement induces non‑canonical MET and Wnt signaling, promoting early bone seeding.
Tumor‑derived JAG1 activates osteoclast maturation and osteoblast cytokine production, establishing a TGF‑β/JAG1 positive feedback loop.
Neutralizing antibody 15d11 targeting JAG1 combined with chemotherapy reduces bone metastasis development by ~100% in mouse models.
DACH1 forms biomolecular condensates that suppress Wnt signaling, mediating the transition from early seeding to osteolytic growth.
IMAC (VK1⁺/CD163⁺/CCR3⁺) macrophages are relocalized to bone metastatic niches, supporting tumor growth and causing anemia by disrupting erythropoiesis islands.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: JAG1 expression in osteoblasts confers chemotherapy resistance to tumor cells.
Bone metastases show depletion of adaptive immune cells (B, T, DC) contributing to immune evasion.

combination_strategies.rationale: JAG1 neutralization sensitizes tumor cells to chemotherapy
combination_strategies.phenotype: Combined treatment nearly abolishes bone metastasis growth

open_questions: Can CCR3 blockade effectively prevent IMAC recruitment without inducing anemia?
What are the precise molecular mechanisms by which DACH1 condensates suppress Wnt signaling?

talk_title: Targeting and detecting the pre‑metastatic niche in brain metastasis

targets.dependency_context: astrocyte‑mediated MHC‑I downregulation in brain metastasis

biomarkers.name: MLIN1
biomarkers.finding: Secreted by astrocytes, upregulates CDK5 leading to MHC‑I downregulation in tumor cells

biomarkers.name: VCAM-1 expression on brain endothelium
biomarkers.finding: Upregulated in pre‑metastatic niche; enables immunoPET detection

models.name: ADORATION trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Phase 1/2 REG inhibitor + radiation in patients with brain metastasis

clinical_data.population: brain metastasis patients in ADORATION trial
brain metastasis patients in ADORATION trial

MOA_summary: Tumor‑derived extracellular vesicles bearing CEACAM1/MIP direct cancer cells to the brain, where they disrupt the BBB via miR‑181c or VEGF‑A, induce astrocyte STAT3‑driven CDK5 upregulation (MLIN1 mediated) to suppress MHC‑I, and create a metabolic niche rich in GABA and glutamate; targeting VCAM‑1 with radiolabeled antibodies enables immunoPET detection and alpha‑particle therapy, while REG inhibition remodels the immune microenvironment, reducing M2 macrophages and MDSCs and enhancing CD8 T‑cell activity.
key_findings: CEACAM1/MIP on tumor EVs serves as a zip code directing metastasis to the brain.
miR‑181c and VEGF‑A from tumor EVs disrupt BBB tight junctions, facilitating tumor cell entry.
Astrocyte‑derived MLIN1 upregulates CDK5, leading to MHC‑I downregulation and immune evasion; rosuvastatin restores antigen presentation.
Neuronal GABA and glutamate fuel brain metastatic tumor cells, promoting proliferation.
Radiolabeled anti‑VCAM‑1 immunoPET detects pre‑metastatic niches before MRI-visible lesions.
REG inhibitor combined with radiation shows 70% partial and 24% complete responses in a Phase 1/2 brain metastasis trial.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Astrocyte‑mediated CDK5 activation creates an immune‑cold niche resistant to anti‑PD‑1 therapy.
REG pathway inhibition overcomes M2 macrophage–driven immunosuppression.

combination_strategies.rationale: REG inhibition sensitizes tumors to radiation‑induced immune activation
combination_strategies.phenotype: High response rates in brain metastasis patients

combination_strategies.rationale: Inhibits astrocyte CDK5 to restore MHC‑I expression
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced T‑cell recognition of brain metastatic cells in preclinical models

open_questions: Can VCAM‑1–targeted alpha‑particle therapy eradicate microscopic pre‑metastatic niches without toxicity?
What are the downstream effectors of REG signaling that mediate MDSC recruitment?
